paper_title: Feeding Preferences and Dental Microwear Analysis of Extinct and Extant Squirrels

methods.description: This method involves measuring and quantifying microwear features on the molars of squirrel specimens to understand their feeding preferences. The features include fine scratches, wide scratches, large pits, and small pits, which are indicative of dietary habits.

steps.description: Select specimens with good preservation of the occlusal surface, avoiding the ones with advanced wear as well as unworn molars.
steps.searchable_summary: select well-preserved molar specimens for analysis

steps.description: Examine a square area of 0.01 mm² using ObjectJ (software Fiji-ImageJ 1.04s) to quantify microwear features.
steps.searchable_summary: use ObjectJ to quantify microwear features on molars

steps.description: Differentiate pits from scratches based on a width/length ratio higher than 1/4.
steps.searchable_summary: differentiate pits from scratches using width/length ratio

steps.description: Distinguish between wide and fine features with a maximum width of 5 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: classify features as wide or fine based on width

steps.description: Measure microwear features from photographs taken with an ESEM at 500x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: measure microwear features using ESEM photographs

methods.description: This method involves statistical analysis of microwear features to explore differences among species and identify features explaining most of the variance.

steps.description: Conduct a Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to identify features explaining most of the variance.
Use a non-parametric Kruskal-Wallis test for independent variables to test differences among species.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis test on species data

steps.description: Perform a pairwise comparison (Wilcoxon test) with Bonferroni correction for the p-values.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Wilcoxon test with Bonferroni correction

methods.description: This method measures the enamel thickness of squirrel teeth using Micro-CT scanning and software analysis to understand dental adaptations.

steps.description: Select unworn specimens with good preservation of the occlusal surface.
steps.searchable_summary: select unworn specimens for enamel measurement

steps.description: Use a Micro-CT scanner Nikon XTH 160 to produce 3D images of the teeth.
steps.searchable_summary: scan teeth with Micro-CT for 3D imaging

steps.description: Measure enamel thickness using VGStudio MAX 3.0.5 software after orienting dental elements.
steps.searchable_summary: measure enamel thickness with VGStudio MAX
